research.product: Okta Workforce Identity Cloud — Groups API (Group Rules)
research.interface: /api/v1/groups/rules, Okta Core management REST API (official OpenAPI spec 2026.07.1)

official_sources.title: okta/okta-management-openapi-spec — Okta Admin Management API (version 2026.07.1)
official_sources.used_for: listGroupRules (limit default 50 max 200, after cursor, search); createGroupRule (type enum group_rule, note 'Group rules are created with the status set to INACTIVE'); replaceGroupRule ('You can only update rules with a group whose status is set to INACTIVE' and 'You currently can't update the actions section'); activate/deactivateGroupRule lifecycle POSTs answering 204; GroupRuleStatus enum ACTIVE/INACTIVE/INVALID with 'You can't update the status of a rule from INACTIVE to ACTIVE — use the lifecycle operations'; GroupRuleExpression 'Only valid value is urn:okta:expression:1.0'; GroupRuleGroupCondition (people.groups) marked 'Currently not supported'; security okta.groups.read/manage

official_sources.title: Core Okta API | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: SSWS Authorization scheme, application/json media types, cursor pagination via Link rel="next"/rel="self" headers with the instruction to follow returned link values because cursor/query formats can change without notice, Okta error envelope fields

official_sources.title: Okta Expression Language overview guide | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: group rule conditions allow only String/Arrays/user expressions; this legacy-EL page is the one group rules bind to (expression type urn:okta:expression:1.0) while Identity Engine features use the separate 'EL in Identity Engine' variant — grounding for rejecting non-1.0 expression types in a reconciler that targets current docs

official_sources.title: Group Rules | Okta Developer (management API reference)
official_sources.used_for: canonical current reference location for the Group Rules operations rendered from the same OpenAPI contract

research.language_rationale: The IT automation repo this reconciler joins is TypeScript on Node (the existing okta/client.ts ships here); Okta documents group rules as plain versioned REST with JSON bodies, Link-header paging, and 204 lifecycle POSTs, so Node's built-in fetch against the documented contract is the credible dependency-free surface (Okta's Node SDK wraps the same OpenAPI spec).

verified_facts: GET /api/v1/groups/rules pages with limit (default 50, max 200) and an opaque after cursor delivered via the Link rel="next" header; the next URL must be followed verbatim
POST /api/v1/groups/rules requires type=group_rule and creates the rule with status INACTIVE; status is not writable on create
PUT /api/v1/groups/rules/{id} is only legal for INACTIVE rules, and the actions section cannot be updated through it
Status changes go exclusively through POST .../lifecycle/activate and .../lifecycle/deactivate, both answering 204 with an empty body
GroupRuleStatus enum is ACTIVE/INACTIVE/INVALID; INVALID is server-assigned
conditions.expression.type has exactly one valid value: urn:okta:expression:1.0
conditions.people.users.exclude is supported; conditions.people.groups.exclude is marked 'Currently not supported' in the current reference
actions.assignUserToGroups.groupIds is the assignment target list; the API expresses no ordering semantics for it
Okta errors use the standard envelope (errorCode/errorSummary/errorLink/errorId/errorCauses), e.g. E0000007 for 404
